assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain: ## Repository (e.g. 'trustify-backend'), ## Target Branch ('main'), ## Description, at least one of ## Files to Modify or ## Files to Create (all 6 have Files to Modify; tasks 1, 3, 6 also have Files to Create), ## Implementation Notes, ## Acceptance Criteria with checklist items, and ## Test Requirements with checklist items. Verified in task-1 through task-6.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 6 task files: task-1-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-advisory-severity-aggregation-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-threshold-query-param.md, task-6-integration-tests.md. 6 is within [3, 10].
All 6 tasks have '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend': task-1 line 4, task-2 line 4, task-3 line 4, task-4 line 4, task-5 line 4, task-6 line 4.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency edges: Task 2 -> Task 1, Task 3 -> Task 2, Task 4 -> Task 3, Task 5 -> Task 3, Task 6 -> Task 3. Task 1 has no dependencies. This forms a tree (a subset of DAG) with no cycles: 1 <- 2 <- 3 <- {4, 5, 6}.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths that exist in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (task 1), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (task 2), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (task 3), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (task 4), tests/Cargo.toml (task 6). Files to Modify referencing files created in earlier tasks: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs and modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (task 5) — these are created in tasks 3 and 1 respectively. Files to Create: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (task 1, in existing sbom/model/ dir), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (task 3, in existing sbom/endpoints/ dir), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (task 6, in existing tests/api/ dir). All paths are within directories shown in the repo structure.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lists 7 specific changes with concrete details: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct with fields: critical, high, medium, low, total', 'Add service method to SbomService that queries the sbom_advisory join table, joins to the advisory table, deduplicates by advisory ID, and aggregates counts by severity level', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler', 'Register the new endpoint route in the SBOM endpoints module with 5-minute tower-http cache configuration', 'Add optional ?threshold query parameter', 'Add cache invalidation hook in the advisory ingestion pipeline', 'Add integration tests for the new endpoint covering: success response shape, 404 for unknown SBOM, deduplication of advisories, cache behavior, and threshold filtering'. All items name specific structs, tables, endpoints, or behaviors.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have '## Repository' followed by exactly 'trustify-backend' on the next line — a single repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: ## Target Branch (set to 'main'), ## Description (with substantive content), ## Acceptance Criteria (with checklist items), ## Test Requirements (with checklist items). Verified across task-1 through task-6.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All 'Files to Modify' paths reference files explicitly listed in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, tests/Cargo.toml. The two paths referencing files created by earlier tasks (advisory_summary.rs in endpoints and model dirs) are in directories that exist in the manifest. 'Files to Create' paths are new files within existing directories from the manifest (sbom/model/, sbom/endpoints/, tests/api/). No invented directory structures.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs' (SbomSummary), 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs' (SbomDetails). Task 2: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs' (SbomService: fetch, list, ingest), 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs'. Task 3: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs'. Task 4: references 'modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs', 'server/src/main.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 5: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs'. Task 6: references 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs'. All use specific file paths and code patterns.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 7, task-2 line 7, task-3 line 7, task-4 line 7, task-5 line 7, task-6 line 7.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'
assertion_results.evidence: No task file in the outputs directory contains any '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:' marker or any SHA-256 hash. Searched all 6 task files and the impact-map.md for 'Description digest', 'sha256:', and 'sdlc-workflow' — no matches found. The digest comments are entirely absent from the output.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task file contains any convention-aware enrichment. There are no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' references, no 'Applies:' rationale lines in any Implementation Notes section. The only references to 'convention' in the outputs are generic phrases like 'consistent with the caching approach documented in the repository conventions' (task-3 line 24) and 'per the project's error handling convention' (task-3 line 26), which are free-form prose rather than the prescribed format. No convention applicability validation is evidenced.
